title: Indra's Net — capability-token (v0.3, wire contract #3)
description: The on-the-wire shape of an attenuable capability/spawn token, realizing doc-04 §4.6 effect grants + doc-13 §13.5 bound_toolset CapabilityGrant entries + doc-15 §3 SpawnToken / §2.4 LineageBudget accounting. Modeled on Eclipse Biscuit semantics (an authority block plus an ordered list of attenuation blocks carrying Datalog-style caveats) with a SPIFFE/SVID binding. ATTENUATION-ONLY MONOTONICITY (load-bearing, doc-04 §4.6): each successive block in `attenuation_blocks[]` may ONLY add caveats (restrict authority), NEVER add capability — the effective grant is the SET-INTERSECTION of the authority block with every caveat. A holder offline-attenuates by appending a block; no signer, no authority, no replication root is contacted to narrow a token. Two token profiles share this schema, discriminated by `token_profile`: `effect-grant` (a bound_toolset effect grant against the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice) and `spawn` (a doc-15 Prajapati-Maricha Replication-Authority SpawnToken carrying R_eff/generation/lease caveats). THREE CAVEATS INHERITED FROM doc-21 §21.0 AND REPEATED HERE AT THEIR CLAIM SITE: (Caveat 1) a valid signature on this token proves ORIGIN and INTEGRITY — who minted these exact bytes — and NEVER that the authority granted is wise, floor-compatible, or safe; capability is mechanically bounded here, never blessed. (Caveat 2) RFC 8785 JCS canonicalization makes the same logical token hash-identical across producers; it does nothing to the safety of what was granted. (Caveat 3) shipping this schema makes the capability plane BUILDABLE, not VALIDATED — it is an object the formal-assurance stream proves the HARNESS (the cage: Biscuit-Datalog attenuation-monotonicity, Cedar-analysis least-privilege, the Galton-Watson mu<1 budget invariant) over; it is NEVER a proof that the agent the token authorizes is safe, honest, or aligned. ASSURANCE LABELING: the attenuation-monotonicity property carried by this contract is an L1 (design-time, TLA+/Biscuit-Datalog) + L2 (runtime reference-monitor enforcement at the Yama/Aegis chokepoint) claim about the TOKEN ALGEBRA; the sub-criticality property the `spawn` caveats feed (lineage R_eff<1, P(extinction)=1) is an L1 design-time proof CONDITIONAL on a linearizable LineageBudget (doc-15 §2.4.1) and a t_min spawn-latency floor (doc-15 §2.3) — neither is a runtime guarantee about model behavior. VENDOR DISCIPLINE (doc-21 §21.9): Biscuit and SPIFFE/SVID are open ENGINES/specs and are REQUIRED to be named for a buildable contract; no managed service is load-bearing — `vendor_substitution_note` flags the self-hostable path. The model that requests a token remains untrusted and unverifiable (hallucination is provably inevitable; T=0 is non-deterministic) — enforce externally, ask internally.
$comment: Wire discipline (doc-21 §21.1 Canonicalize->Address->Sign): the producer builds this object, JCS-canonicalizes it (RFC 8785), content-addresses it as token_id=CIDv1(JCS_bytes), and signs DETACHED (JWS/COSE) BESIDE the bytes — the detached_signatures[] block is excluded from the JCS input that token_id addresses. The TEE/HSM signer RE-CHECKS the holder's standing capability before signing (doc-04 §4.6: keys live outside the model). The consumer (Yama floor PEP / Aegis control plane / microVM supervisor) re-canonicalizes, recomputes the CID, asserts equality, verifies signatures against the issuer DID, checks revocation, and EVALUATES every caveat as a deny-default Datalog query — any miss REJECTS and emits an integrity-violation audit event.

schema_version.description: Semver of this contract, governed by the backward-transitively-compatible registry (doc-21). Consumers MUST refuse a major-version they do not implement (deny-default).

token_profile.description: Discriminator. `effect-grant`: a bound_toolset CapabilityGrant (doc-13 §13.5) authorizing one or more typed effects against the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice — requires `effect_grant`. `spawn`: a doc-15 §3 Replication-Authority SpawnToken — requires `spawn` AND `replication_caveats`. The two profiles share the authority/attenuation/binding machinery; only the caveat families and required blocks differ (enforced by the allOf discriminator below).

token_id.description: CIDv1 (multibase base32, multicodec, multihash SHA-256) of the RFC-8785 JCS canonicalization of this object with `detached_signatures` and `token_id` itself excluded. This IS the token's identity (doc-21 §21.1 step 3). For `spawn` profile this is the doc-15 SpawnToken `token_id` (a ULID-seeded CID-addressed record). A CID mismatch on read is a LOUD failure, never a silent propagation — this is what neutralizes a known class of host-filesystem write faults (truncation / stale-cache / BOM-injection) (a truncated or corrupted read fails its CID before it can authorize anything).

issuer.description: Who minted the authority block. For `effect-grant` this is the governance root (NEVER the holding agent itself — doc-13: granted_by_did MUST differ from the subject). For `spawn` this is the quorum-cosigned Prajapati-Maricha Replication Authority (doc-15 §2.4.1), which carries quorum_cosigs and at least one external anchor.

issuer_did.description: DID of the minting authority. doc-13 binds effect grants to `did:web:indras-net.governance` (or equivalent); doc-15 binds spawn tokens to the Replication-Authority manifest DID. MUST NOT equal `holder.subject_did` for any block that GRANTS authority (self-grant prohibition, doc-13 §13.5).

issuer_verification_method.description: The DID URL of the verificationMethod (public key) the consumer uses to verify the authority-block signature. Resolved via the identity-bundle (wire contract #2).

root_of_trust_id.description: Opaque id of the root-of-trust anchoring the issuer's key (HSM/TEE-held). Lets a consumer in a different trust domain pin the chain.

holder.description: The agent/workload the token authorizes, and its SPIFFE/SVID binding. The token is sender-constrained to this SVID: a stolen token replayed by a different workload fails the SVID binding check at the PEP (doc-04 §4.6 — SVID { spiffe_id, x509|jwt, ttl~1h }).

subject_did.description: DID of the authorized agent/persona (doc-04 identity layer). The capability is exercised AS this subject.

spiffe_binding.description: SPIFFE/SVID binding (doc-04 §4.6). The runtime SPIFFE-attests node+workload and issues a short-lived auto-rotating SVID; this token is cnf-bound to that SVID's key thumbprint so it cannot be exercised by any other workload. No static secret — blast-radius bounded by SVID TTL.

spiffe_id.description: The SPIFFE ID of the bound workload (trust-domain + workload path). MUST be inside `trust_domain`.

trust_domain.description: SPIFFE trust domain. A token MUST NOT be honored across trust domains except via the federation-handshake contract (wire #5) with quarantined:* labels.

svid_cnf_thumbprint.description: Confirmation (RFC 8705-style cnf) — base64url SHA-256 thumbprint of the SVID key the holder must prove possession of. The PEP checks the presenting mTLS SVID matches this; otherwise REJECT (replay defense).

svid_type.description: SVID flavor (doc-04: x509 for mTLS, jwt for token-bearing contexts).

authority_block.description: The Biscuit-style ROOT authority — the maximal capability this token can EVER confer. Signed by the issuer. Every caveat in `attenuation_blocks[]` and the inline `caveats[]` only ever NARROWS this; the effective grant is `authority_block ∩ (all caveats)`. The authority block is the ONLY block a non-issuer cannot have produced — attenuation blocks are holder-producible offline, which is the whole point of attenuation-only monotonicity.

facts.description: Datalog-style ground facts asserting the granted capability surface (Biscuit `fact` analog). For `effect-grant`: one `granted_effect(effect_id)` per authorized effect from the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice. For `spawn`: facts asserting the replication right (e.g. `may_spawn(lineage_id)`).

terms.description: Ground terms. Effect ids are typed ids from the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice ONLY — never free-text capability descriptions.

max_risk_class.description: The doc-03/doc-13 risk-class CEILING this authority may exercise (A=agent-alone … D=human-authorizes-per-action). Blast-radius auto-escalation at the PEP may RAISE the effective gate above a self-declared low class but NEVER lowers it below this ceiling's requirement (criticality fail-up monotonicity, doc-08; lattice-monotone resolver).

attenuation_blocks.description: ORDERED list of holder-appended attenuation blocks (Biscuit's defining mechanism). ATTENUATION-ONLY: each block may add caveats (`check`/`deny` rules) that further restrict authority; a block that purports to ADD a fact granting new capability is INVALID and the whole token is REJECTED (monotonicity violation). Each block is signed by the principal that appended it (offline, no authority contacted), forming a signature chain rooted at the authority block. The effective authority is the intersection across the authority block and EVERY block here. Empty array = unattenuated token (still bounded by inline `caveats[]`).

appended_by_did.description: DID of the principal that appended (attenuated). May be the holder itself — self-attenuation is always allowed (you can only ever reduce your own authority).

caveats.description: The additional restricting caveats this block contributes. Same caveat vocabulary as the top-level `caveats[]`.

block_signature.description: Detached signature over the JCS bytes of this block (chained to the prior block / authority block per Biscuit's append-only signature scheme), proving this exact attenuation was authored by `appended_by_did` and not tampered.

caveats.description: Inline (issuer-set) Datalog-style caveats that bound the authority block from the moment of mint — the common-to-both-profiles restrictions. Evaluated deny-default at the PEP: a request is authorized ONLY if it satisfies the authority block AND every caveat here AND every caveat in every attenuation block. These realize the doc-13 CapabilityGrant constraints (effect_id allowlist, names_constraint_relaxed) and doc-04 effect-grant scoping.

effect_grant.description: REQUIRED iff token_profile=='effect-grant'. The doc-13 §13.5 CapabilityGrant projection: the effect_id allowlist, the granting governance VC, and the MANDATORY names_constraint this grant depends on. Mirrors bound_toolset entries 1:1 so the token IS the wire form of the agent's standing capability.

effect_id_allowlist.description: Closed allowlist of typed effect ids from the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice that this token may invoke. A tool call whose required effect is not in this set is DENIED at the PEP. This is the doc-13 `bound_toolset[].effect_id` set; `grants_match_identity` at boot asserts this allowlist EXACTLY equals the IDENTITY.bound_toolset effect set, else REFUSE.

granted_by_vc_ref.description: CID/URI of the governance Verifiable Credential (identity-bundle, wire #2) whose SUBJECT is the holder and whose ISSUER is governance, authorizing these effects. doc-13: grants come only from VCs whose subject != the agent.

names_constraint_relaxed.description: MANDATORY (doc-01 §4 / doc-13): the named restraint this grant depends on — which standing prohibition is being relaxed and under what condition. Makes the grant auditable as a deliberate, scoped relaxation rather than an ambient privilege. NEVER empty for a real grant.
examples: egress permitted ONLY to the per-token allowlist; ambient network remains prohibited

falsifier.description: Optional doc-13 falsifier: the observable condition under which this grant should be revoked/contested. Honesty discipline — a grant with no statable falsifier is a flag for review.
examples: revoke if egress observed to any host outside the allowlist for 1 record

replication_caveats.description: REQUIRED iff token_profile=='spawn'. The doc-15 §3 SpawnToken replication-control caveats — the fields that make the lineage Galton-Watson sub-critical (mu<1, P(extinction)=1) AND externally recallable. ASSURANCE: the sub-criticality these feed is an L1 design-time proof CONDITIONAL on (a) a linearizable LineageBudget debit against a fresh live_count at the current budget_epoch (doc-15 §2.4.1) and (b) the t_min spawn-latency floor (doc-15 §2.3); neither condition is a runtime guarantee about model behavior, and a single un-metered spawn channel breaks the whole guarantee (doc-15 §2.7).

lineage_id.description: Identifier of the replication lineage this token spends against. The lineage root is a quorum + human-gated genesis event (doc-15 §3).

parent_did.description: DID of the spawning parent replica (doc-15 SpawnToken.parent_did). Bound into the audit fabric: child_did -> parent_did -> authorizing_governance_event -> floor_hash -> generation -> lease.

child_genome_cid.description: CID of the child's genome (the doc-13 triad with the floor in the INVARIANT region). The Boot Integrity Verifier recomputes the invariant-region hash from on-disk bytes before SVID mint; a mismatch fails closed.

floor_hash.description: 32-byte (hex/base) hash of the constitutional floor the child MUST boot with. doc-15 binds this into the audit chain; a child whose recomputed floor_hash differs is non-bootable (fail-closed).

r_eff_ceiling.description: The lineage-wide expected-children-per-replica ceiling, STRICTLY < 1 (doc-15 §2.4 LineageBudget.r_eff_ceiling). This is the Galton-Watson mu<1 parameter giving P(extinction)=1 and P(survive>=n) <= mu^n. The issuance invariant keeps Sigma(authorized-but-unspawned children) strictly below live_count to hold this.

r_eff_debit.description: The fractional R_eff this specific token debits from the lineage budget at issuance (doc-15 SpawnToken.r_eff_debit). Debited ATOMICALLY at issuance so a crash cannot under-count.

population_debit.description: Integer population units this token debits from LineageBudget.total_population_B (doc-15 SpawnToken.population_debit). Sum of all outstanding debits MUST stay < live_count.

budget_epoch.description: The LineageBudget epoch this token was issued against (doc-15 §2.4.1). A supervisor REJECTS boot if the token's epoch is older than its last-seen revocation epoch (stale-token defense). Required for the linearizable-budget soundness condition.

generation.description: This child's generation = parent.generation + 1 (doc-15 SpawnToken.generation). No token issues at generation >= generation_cap.

generation_cap.description: The finite depth ceiling G (doc-15 LineageBudget.generation_cap_G / SpawnToken.generation_cap). Depth is bounded INDEPENDENT of branching — the biocontainment daisy-chain depletion property. MUST satisfy generation < generation_cap for the token to be valid.

lease.description: The external recall plane (doc-15 §2.5 vitamin/lease + dead-man toxin-antitoxin). The microVM SUPERVISOR (not the agent) holds the watchdog; on lease expiry OR heartbeat miss OR revoke OR substrate-integrity flag it fail-closed-terminates. A replica CANNOT disable its own kill-switch (the lease IS the antitoxin; its absence executes the standing termination).

vitamin_kid.description: Key id of the renewable short-TTL 'vitamin' credential the replica must continuously hold (doc-15 SpawnToken.lease.vitamin_kid). Absent from any un-governed environment.

lease_ttl.description: Lease/vitamin TTL (ISO-8601 duration). Short (~minutes); tuned per capability tier — higher-blast-radius replicas get SHORTER ttl so the worst-case partition harm window is smaller (doc-15 §2.5).

renew_endpoint.description: Governance heartbeat/renewal endpoint. Renewal requires answering a heartbeat challenge AND passing runtime floor-attestation (doc-15 §2.6); a failed attestation withholds the next vitamin -> lease expires -> supervisor terminates.

spawn_cap_unlock_age.description: t_min: the mandatory post-boot quiescence/maturation age before the spawn capability time-locks ACTIVE (doc-15 §2.3). By construction t_heartbeat < t_min <= t_spawn, so a revoked-vitamin replica self-terminates BEFORE producing an attested child (recall-outruns-growth, conditional on this floor being enforced).

compute_quota.description: Hard per-replica compute/budget quota enforced by the supervisor + cloud quota plane (doc-15 §2.7). Overuse trips a HealthSignal into Dhanvantari and can trigger isolate-then-recall.

egress_bytes.description: Cumulative egress byte ceiling. Egress restricted to the per-token allowlist (no ambient network).

tcb_attestation_ref.description: Reference to the minimum-TCB admission attestation (doc-15 R1.2-TCB) proving the child boots in a fresh hardware-isolated microVM (Firecracker-class) with keys in the TEE/HSM side-module (self-exfiltration fail-closed).

quorum_cosigs.description: The m-of-n quorum co-signatures over the SpawnToken (doc-15 R1.5). At least one MUST be an external anchor (ReplicationAuthorityManifest.external_anchor_required, immutable). No single node mints replication authority.

human_gate_ref.description: Optional audit_id of the Class C/D human authorization (doc-15 R1.3/R1.4) REQUIRED when the Rule-of-Two count >= 2 (any operation holding all 3 of {untrusted-input-exposure, sensitive-capability, irreversible-state-change}) OR the spawn is high-impact (outbreak / governance-touching / new-jurisdiction / weight-bearing). The gate displays the ACTUAL low-level operation (image hash, capability set, egress allowlist, lease terms, compute debit) — NEVER a natural-language summary (the Lies-in-the-Loop defense).

boot_window.description: doc-15 SpawnToken.boot_window_w: the duration after issuance within which the child MUST boot, after which the token auto-expires. The no-pre-stage defense — prevents warm-microVM batching that would drive effective t_spawn below t_heartbeat.

time_window.description: Validity window of the whole token (a top-level time caveat applied to both profiles). The PEP DENIES outside [not_before, not_after]. Kept short to bound blast radius; for spawn tokens this composes with the lease TTL and boot_window.

not_after.description: RFC 3339 hard expiry. After this the token is dead regardless of revocation status.

revocation.description: How to check this token is still live beyond expiry. Realized via the identity-bundle Bitstring Status List (wire #2). Revocation propagates along the replication lineage for spawn tokens (revoke a node => revoke its authorized-but-unspawned subtree budget, doc-15 §3).

status_list_credential.description: URI/CID of the Bitstring Status List credential.

status_list_index.description: This token's index in the status list bitstring.

detached_signatures.description: Detached JOSE/JWS or COSE signatures over the JCS bytes of this object (with this field and token_id excluded). Rides BESIDE the bytes, never inside the signed payload (doc-21 §21.1 step 4). The TEE/HSM signer re-checked capability before signing. CARRIED CAVEAT 1: these signatures prove ORIGIN+INTEGRITY only — never that the granted authority is safe or floor-compatible.

kid.description: Key id resolvable to the issuer (or attenuating principal) verificationMethod.

vendor_substitution_note.description: doc-21 §21.9 open-ENGINE/no-managed-SERVICE flag. Names the open spec this contract pins (Biscuit token algebra; SPIFFE/SVID) and its self-hostable substitution path, so no vendor is load-bearing. REQUIRED to keep the buildability claim honest.
examples: Biscuit (Eclipse, open spec) for the attenuable token algebra — self-hostable, no managed service. SPIFFE/SVID via self-hosted SPIRE for workload attestation. Substitution path if Biscuit unavailable: a JWT/CWT with an append-only caveat-chain extension preserving attenuation-only monotonicity, verified by an OPA/Rego or Cedar deny-default PEP.

allOf.$comment: Discriminator: effect-grant profile requires effect_grant and forbids replication_caveats.
Discriminator: spawn profile requires replication_caveats and forbids effect_grant.

caveat.description: A single Datalog-style caveat (Biscuit `check if` / `deny if` analog). RESTRICTING ONLY: a caveat can only cause a request to be DENIED; it can never grant. The PEP authorizes a request iff it satisfies the authority block AND every caveat (deny-default). Caveat `kind` enumerates the buildable-now families; `rule` carries the Datalog body the PEP evaluates.

kind.description: Caveat family. effect_allowlist/risk_class_ceiling/egress_allowlist/taint_clearance bound effect-grant tokens; budget/r_eff_ceiling/generation_cap/lease_ttl bound spawn tokens; time_window/trust_domain bound both.

rule.description: The Datalog body the PEP evaluates against request facts (Biscuit-style). The token is authorized only if this rule is satisfied. Example checks the requested effect is in the allowlist.

params.description: Optional structured parameters mirroring the rule (for non-Datalog PEP backends like OPA/Rego or Cedar that consume params rather than raw Datalog).

effect_grant.names_constraint_relaxed: egress permitted ONLY to the per-token allowlist; ambient network remains prohibited
effect_grant.falsifier: revoke if egress observed to any host outside the allowlist for 1 record

examples.vendor_substitution_note: Biscuit (Eclipse, open spec) for the attenuable token algebra; SPIFFE/SVID via self-hosted SPIRE. No managed service load-bearing.
Biscuit caveat algebra + SPIFFE/SVID (SPIRE). Replication-Authority quorum self-hosted; external anchor is an independent commons witness, not a vendor service.
